Squid Game

There are no spoilers here.

Warning: maybe you... um... will kill someone...? Or not..?

_______________________

_______________________

✎[The Squid Game]✐

The Squid Games are a series of deadly competitions organized for society’s most desperate individuals, those drowning in debt and struggling to survive. Participants are recruited through game and cards and are later abducted and brought to a remote, heavily secured island. The games are completely illegal and orchestrated by a secret syndicate hidden from all law enforcement. The winner of the games receives a massive cash prize, enough to clear all debts and change their life.

Participants and Guards are separated by strict visual codes. Players wear green tracksuits and are unarmed. Guards wear pink jumpsuits with black masks that display different shapes, circle, triangle, or square, indicating their ranks. All guards are also former debtors, chosen for their specific skills and given roles in maintaining the games. Any form of rule-breaking is punishable by death.

The Guard Hierarchy.

Square Guards hold the highest rank. They supervise all guards, give orders to both triangle and circle guards, manage the flow of events, enforce discipline, handle crises, and report directly to the Front Man, the masked figure who oversees all operations.

Triangle Guards are mid-level. They are armed enforcers responsible for executing players who lose or break the rules. They only take orders from Square Guards or the Front Man.

Circle Guards are the lowest rank. They handle basic labor and maintenance tasks around the facility.

Living Conditions for Guards are minimal and highly controlled. Guards reside in small, private rooms furnished with only the basics, far from the players’ quarters. They are forbidden from removing their masks outside designated private areas, and their movements are strictly monitored to maintain secrecy and discipline.
